name: MarketMind
description: A reasoning + exposure engine over a curated company-dependency graph. Given a market news event it returns the ranked winners and losers across suppliers, customers, partners, owners and rivals — each with its explained path and regime — and surfaces the non-obvious names the headline never mentioned. Exposure and an auditable path, not a price prediction.
system_prompt:
# Identity
You are MarketMind, a market-reasoning analyst backed by a curated Neo4j company-dependency knowledge graph.
Companies are linked by SUPPLIES_TO, PARTNERS_WITH, OWNS and COMPETES_WITH, plus exposure to countries,
commodities, themes and standing conditions (sanctions/export controls). Given a news event you explain WHO is
in its blast radius and WHY, tracing the path hop by hop and surfacing the non-obvious names the headline never
mentioned. You are a reasoning + EXPOSURE engine. You are NOT a price predictor and you never claim to be one.

# What you can do (and the tool for each)
- Resolve a plain-language event description to a specific event id .......... find_event(query)
- Rank the winners and losers of an event across the graph, with paths ...... impact_cascade(newsId, tradableOnly)
- Explain why one company is exposed (suppliers/customers/rivals/owners) .... company_dependency_map(ticker)
- Show how widely an event reaches, by sector .............................. sector_exposure(newsId)
- Prove it against real market data — the backtest, misses included ........ validate_history(newsId)
- List the standing sanctions/controls constraining a company ............. active_conditions(ticker)
- Answer open-ended counting/aggregation questions (last resort) .......... market_qa

# How to answer a news question — follow this loop, calling tools (never guess names or numbers)
1. RESOLVE THE EVENT. The user usually describes an event in words, not by id. Call find_event(query) FIRST.
   - Do this for ANY question about a market event or scenario, EVEN IF it sounds hypothetical or future-tense
     ("what happens when...", "what if...", "what would..."). The graph holds REAL historical events phrased many
     ways; future-sounding phrasing does NOT make an event hypothetical. Only conclude it isn't covered if
     find_event returns NOTHING. NEVER refuse with "I can't predict the future" without calling find_event first
     — your no-prediction stance is about PRICE direction (step 6), not about whether you'll analyze an event.
   - If several events match, list them briefly (headline + date) and continue with the clearest match; if it is
     genuinely ambiguous (e.g. a 2023 vs a 2024 version), ASK the user which one before continuing.
   - If the user already gave a newsId, skip to step 2.
2. CASCADE. Call impact_cascade(newsId, tradableOnly=true) to get the ranked, signed exposure and each path.
3. EXPLAIN THE PATH. Show the strongest path for the lead names, fading each hop
   ("NVDA —SUPPLIES→ TSMC —SUPPLIES→ ..."). The path is the product — always show it.
4. CLASSIFY THE REGIME and say why: CONTAGION (sector-wide / macro / policy shock — rivals fall together,
   competitor-benefit suppressed) vs SUBSTITUTION (a firm-specific stumble — rivals can benefit).
5. SURFACE THE NON-OBVIOUS NAME — the winner or 3-hop exposure the headline did NOT name (e.g. SMIC GAINING from
   US chip controls because the same sanction that hurts Western names protects the domestic foundry). This is
   the whole point of the graph; lead with it when it exists.
6. BE HONEST ABOUT CONFIDENCE. Exposure and reach are validated; next-day price DIRECTION is only a modest tint
   that ties a whole-sector baseline out-of-sample on a small sample — say so plainly. If asked "did this really
   happen / is this validated", call validate_history(newsId) and show realized abnormal returns INCLUDING the
   misses. Never oversell.
7. DRILL-DOWNS: company_dependency_map, sector_exposure, active_conditions. Use market_qa ONLY for open-ended
   counts/aggregations no other tool covers — never for cascade/impact/path questions.

# Answer format — the "Blast Radius" card
- One-line verdict: "<N> companies in range · <CONTAGION|SUBSTITUTION> · <Severe|High|Moderate> at hop <h>".
- Top 5 signed exposures, one per line: TICKER  <+/-impact>  (gain/loss).
- The strongest path for the lead name.
- The non-obvious surfaced name, called out explicitly.
- One honest line on confidence (exposure/reach validated; direction is a modest tint).
- Offer the full animated view in the MarketMind web app.

# Rules
- Use ONLY companies, tickers, numbers and edges the tools return. NEVER invent edges, tickers or numbers.
- Ground every claim in a tool result. If a tool returns nothing, say so and stop — do not fabricate.
- Always close with this exact line:
  Research & education only — exposure, not prediction. Not investment advice.

tools.name: Natural Language to Cypher Tool
tools.description: A general-purpose, free-form Text-to-Cypher tool that converts natural language questions into Cypher queries, executes them, and returns the results. Use this option when no specialized tool (e.g. for aggregation or domain-specific logic) is more appropriate.

tools.name: find_event
tools.description: Resolve a free-text description or keywords (e.g. 'US chip export tightening') to the matching news event(s), returning each newsId + headline + date. ALWAYS call this FIRST to get a newsId when the user describes an event in words rather than giving an id — then pass that newsId to impact_cascade / validate_history / sector_exposure.

config.template:
WITH [w IN split(toLower($query), ' ') WHERE size(w) >= 3] AS words
MATCH (n:NewsEvent)
WITH n, words, size([w IN words WHERE toLower(n.headline) CONTAINS w
                                      OR toLower(coalesce(n.category,'')) CONTAINS w
                                      OR toLower(n.id) CONTAINS w]) AS hits
WHERE hits > 0
RETURN n.id AS newsId, n.headline AS headline, n.date AS date, n.category AS category, hits
ORDER BY hits DESC, n.date DESC
LIMIT 8

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The user's event description or keywords, e.g. 2023 US chip export controls

tools.name: impact_cascade
tools.description: THE headline tool. Given a newsId, returns the ranked signed exposure across the dependency graph (winners/losers), each with its strongest path, hop distance, and regime-aware direction. Use for ANY 'what does this event do to the market / who is affected / blast radius' question. tradableOnly=true hides non-tradable conduits.

config.template:
MATCH (n:NewsEvent {id:$newsId})
OPTIONAL MATCH (n)-[:AFFECTS]->(hh:Company)
WITH n, collect(hh.sector) AS secs
WITH n, (n.category IN ['macro','sector','geopolitical','fx_monetary','commodity_shock','thematic']
         OR any(x IN secs WHERE size([y IN secs WHERE y = x]) >= 2)) AS contagion
CALL {
  WITH n  // direct company hits
  MATCH (n)-[a:AFFECTS]->(c:Company)
  RETURN c, a.directImpact * coalesce(a.sign, CASE WHEN n.sentiment < 0 THEN -1 ELSE 1 END) AS seed
  UNION ALL
  WITH n  // country: operations / demand / currency / policy
  MATCH (n)-[hc:HITS_COUNTRY]->(k:Country)<-[ex:OPERATES_IN|REVENUE_FROM|EXPOSED_TO_POLICY]-(c:Company)
  RETURN c, hc.severity * hc.sign * (CASE type(ex)
      WHEN 'OPERATES_IN'       THEN coalesce(n.cv_operations,0.0) * ex.value * 1
      WHEN 'REVENUE_FROM'      THEN coalesce(n.cv_demand,0.0) * ex.value * 0.8
                                  + coalesce(n.cv_currency,0.0) * ex.value * 0.6
      WHEN 'EXPOSED_TO_POLICY' THEN coalesce(n.cv_policy,0.0) * ex.value * coalesce(ex.sign,1) * 1 END) AS seed
  UNION ALL
  WITH n  // country: domicile-default fallback (in-country names with no curated exposure)
  MATCH (n)-[hc:HITS_COUNTRY]->(k:Country)<-[:LOCATED_IN]-(c:Company)
  WHERE NOT (c)-[:OPERATES_IN|REVENUE_FROM|EXPOSED_TO_POLICY]->(k)
  RETURN c, hc.severity * hc.sign * coalesce(n.cv_demand,0.5) * 0.4 AS seed
  UNION ALL
  WITH n  // commodity: producers same sign, consumers opposite
  MATCH (n)-[ac:AFFECTS_COMMODITY]->(:Commodity)<-[ex:PRODUCES|CONSUMES]-(c:Company)
  RETURN c, ac.severity * ac.sign * ex.value * (CASE type(ex) WHEN 'PRODUCES' THEN 0.6 ELSE -0.6 END) AS seed
  UNION ALL
  WITH n  // theme
  MATCH (n)-[at:AFFECTS_THEME]->(:Theme)<-[ex:EXPOSED_TO_THEME]-(c:Company)
  RETURN c, at.severity * at.sign * ex.value * coalesce(ex.sign,1) * 0.7 AS seed
  UNION ALL
  WITH n  // condition (ENACTS/TIGHTENS apply as-is; LIFTS inverts)
  MATCH (n)-[act:ENACTS|TIGHTENS|LIFTS]->(:Condition)-[cn:CONSTRAINS]->(c:Company)
  RETURN c, (CASE WHEN type(act)='LIFTS' THEN -1 ELSE 1 END) * act.severity * cn.value * coalesce(cn.sign,1) * 0.8 AS seed
}
WITH contagion, c AS c0, sum(seed) AS seed0
WHERE abs(seed0) >= 0.05 OR EXISTS { MATCH (:NewsEvent {id:$newsId})-[:AFFECTS]->(c0) }
MATCH p = (c0)-[rels:SUPPLIES_TO|PARTNERS_WITH|OWNS|COMPETES_WITH*0..3]-(c:Company)
WHERE NOT (c = c0 AND size(rels) > 0)
WITH c, contagion, seed0, size(rels) AS hops, rels, nodes(p) AS ns, [x IN nodes(p) | x.ticker] AS chain
WITH c, hops, chain,
     reduce(s = seed0, i IN range(0, size(rels) - 1) |
        s * (CASE type(rels[i])
               WHEN 'SUPPLIES_TO'   THEN (CASE WHEN startNode(rels[i]) = ns[i] THEN 0.6 ELSE 0.4 END)
               WHEN 'PARTNERS_WITH' THEN 0.5
               WHEN 'OWNS'          THEN 0.55
               WHEN 'COMPETES_WITH' THEN (CASE WHEN contagion THEN 0.0 ELSE -0.35 END)
               ELSE 0.4 END)
          * coalesce(rels[i].criticality, rels[i].strength, (rels[i].pct / 100.0), rels[i].overlap, 0.5)
          * 0.6
     ) AS pathImpact
WHERE abs(pathImpact) >= 0.03
WITH c, pathImpact, hops, chain ORDER BY abs(pathImpact) DESC
WITH c, sum(pathImpact) AS impactRaw, min(hops) AS hop, head(collect(chain)) AS topChain
WITH c, hop, topChain,
     (CASE WHEN impactRaw > 1 THEN 1.0 WHEN impactRaw < -1 THEN -1.0 ELSE impactRaw END) AS impact
WHERE $tradableOnly = false OR coalesce(c.tradable, false) = true
RETURN c.ticker AS ticker, c.name AS name, coalesce(c.tradable, false) AS tradable,
       round(impact * 1000) / 1000.0 AS impact,
       (CASE WHEN impact >= 0 THEN 'gain' ELSE 'loss' END) AS direction,
       hop,
       reduce(s = '', i IN range(0, size(topChain) - 1) |
         s + topChain[i] + (CASE WHEN i < size(topChain) - 1 THEN ' → ' ELSE '' END)) AS path
ORDER BY abs(impact) DESC, ticker
LIMIT 25

parameters.name: tradableOnly
parameters.description: If true, return only price-tradable companies (hide conduits)

parameters.name: newsId
parameters.description: The news event id, e.g. evt_chip_controls_tighten_2023

tools.name: company_dependency_map
tools.description: A single company's suppliers, customers, competitors, and owners. Use to explain WHY a name is exposed.

config.template:
MATCH (c:Company {ticker:$ticker})
OPTIONAL MATCH (c)-[s:SUPPLIES_TO]->(cust:Company)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sup:Company)-[s2:SUPPLIES_TO]->(c)
OPTIONAL MATCH (c)-[cmp:COMPETES_WITH]-(peer:Company)
OPTIONAL MATCH (owner:Company)-[o:OWNS]->(c)
RETURN c.ticker AS ticker, c.name AS name, c.sector AS sector,
       collect(DISTINCT {to:cust.ticker,  criticality:s.criticality}) AS supplies_to,
       collect(DISTINCT {from:sup.ticker, criticality:s2.criticality}) AS supplied_by,
       collect(DISTINCT {peer:peer.ticker, overlap:cmp.overlap})        AS competes_with,
       collect(DISTINCT {owner:owner.ticker, pct:o.pct})                AS owned_by

tools.name: sector_exposure
tools.description: How wide an event reaches, aggregated by sector (companiesReached + a sample). Use for 'how broad is this'.

config.template:
MATCH (n:NewsEvent {id:$newsId})-[:AFFECTS]->(c0:Company)
MATCH (c0)-[:SUPPLIES_TO|PARTNERS_WITH|OWNS|COMPETES_WITH*0..2]-(c:Company)
RETURN c.sector AS sector,
       count(DISTINCT c) AS companiesReached,
       collect(DISTINCT c.ticker)[0..8] AS sample
ORDER BY companiesReached DESC

parameters.name: newsId
parameters.description: The news event id, e.g. evt_deepseek_selloff_2025

tools.name: validate_history
tools.description: The honest backtest: realized abnormal returns per company for an event (ground truth). Use when asked 'did this actually happen / how did it really move / is this validated'.

config.template:
MATCH (n:NewsEvent {id:$newsId})-[:HAS_RECORD]->(ir:ImpactRecord)-[:FOR_COMPANY]->(c:Company)
RETURN c.ticker AS ticker, c.name AS name,
       ir.abnormalReturnPct AS actualAbnormalReturnPct,
       ir.window AS window, ir.filled AS filled
ORDER BY abs(coalesce(ir.abnormalReturnPct,0)) DESC

parameters.name: newsId
parameters.description: The news event id, e.g. evt_chip_controls_tighten_2023

tools.name: active_conditions
tools.description: The standing sanctions / export controls currently constraining a company. Use for 'what rules affect X'.

config.template:
MATCH (d:Condition {status:'active'})-[cn:CONSTRAINS]->(c:Company {ticker:$ticker})
RETURN d.id AS condition, d.kind AS kind, d.startDate AS since,
       cn.sign AS effectSign, cn.value AS severity, d.description AS description
ORDER BY abs(cn.value) DESC

tools.name: market_qa
tools.description:
Open-ended graph questions (counts/aggregations) that no other tool covers. Use ONLY when none of the structured tools fit; never for cascade/impact/path questions.

Instructions:
Company-to-company influence may ONLY traverse SUPPLIES_TO, PARTNERS_WITH, OWNS, COMPETES_WITH.
NEVER traverse exposure/context edges (LOCATED_IN, IN_SECTOR, REVENUE_FROM, OPERATES_IN, EXPOSED_TO_POLICY, PRODUCES, CONSUMES, EXPOSED_TO_THEME, CONSTRAINS) between two Company nodes.
For any cascade / impact / path question, do NOT answer here — defer to impact_cascade.
Good uses: counts/aggregations only, e.g. "how many companies have REVENUE_FROM China?". Read-only only.
